name: DNDMind AI Copilot
description: Full-stack AI Dungeon Master copilot with RAG, memory retrieval, structured outputs, tool calling, and deterministic evaluation workflows.
problem: Tabletop role-playing sessions create a lot of changing context: character state, campaign memory, rules references, prior decisions, and narrative continuity. A simple chatbot can respond creatively, but it does not reliably preserve memory, retrieve relevant context, or keep outputs structured enough for repeatable gameplay support.
solution: Built a full-stack AI Dungeon Master copilot using Gemini 2.5 Flash for generation, plus application workflow, backend APIs, retrieval, memory, and structured AI responses. The system uses a Next.js interface, ASP.NET Core services, FastAPI inference support, PostgreSQL with pgvector, Docker Compose, embeddings, memory retrieval, tool/function calling, and deterministic evaluation workflows.
proof: Implements RAG and vector retrieval through PostgreSQL + pgvector instead of relying only on prompt history.
Separates frontend, backend, inference, and database responsibilities across a multi-service Docker Compose setup.
Uses Gemini 2.5 Flash with structured AI outputs and tool/function calling patterns to make responses easier to validate and integrate.
result: The project demonstrates production-style AI application architecture beyond a prompt-only demo: persistent memory, retrieval, structured outputs, backend orchestration, and evaluation-ready workflows.

name: Enterprise RAG Knowledge Assistant
description: On-premise LLM assistant redesigned into a retrieval pipeline with document chunking, embeddings, vector retrieval, contextual prompts, and output handling.

screenshots.alt: Enterprise RAG assistant showing retrieved IT security policy document

problem: Internal operational knowledge was spread across documents, systems, and teams. A generic assistant could answer questions, but without retrieval grounding it risked incomplete context, inconsistent answers, and hallucination in an enterprise environment where reliability matters.
solution: Architected an on-premise Qwen-powered internal knowledge assistant and redesigned it into a RAG pipeline. The system uses document chunking, embedding generation, vector retrieval, contextual prompt orchestration, backend inference services, and enterprise UI integration.
proof: Designed the assistant for an on-premise enterprise environment rather than a public-only API workflow.
Moved from simple prompting toward retrieval-grounded answers using chunking, embeddings, vector retrieval, and context assembly.
Used Qwen with controlled response/output handling to reduce hallucination risk and make answers easier to integrate into internal workflows.
result: Improved the architecture of an internal AI assistant from general chat behavior into a more reliable enterprise knowledge workflow with retrieval grounding and clearer backend integration boundaries.

name: NR EMAS Risk Monitoring
description: Enterprise risk evaluation and monitoring platform replacing Excel-based tracking with risk registers, treatment plans, KRI monitoring, approvals, and executive dashboards.

screenshots.alt: NR EMAS dashboard showing risk exposure and risk treatment monitoring
NR EMAS traffic light monitoring table and issues next steps panel

problem: Enterprise risk tracking was fragmented across Excel files and manual reporting flows. Risk registers, treatment plans, KRI monitoring, action plans, approvals, and dashboard reporting needed a centralized system so risk owners and executives could work from the same operational source.
solution: Built NR EMAS as an ASP.NET Core platform using Clean Architecture, separating domain rules, application workflows, infrastructure, and presentation concerns. The system centralizes risk registers and treatment plans, supports action plans with multi-level approval workflows, monitors KRIs and treatment progress, and presents real-time dashboards for executive visibility.
proof: Public Rewire success story describes NR EMAS as replacing Excel-based risk tracking with a centralized workflow-driven platform.
Implemented with ASP.NET Core and Clean Architecture to keep risk domain logic, workflow orchestration, data access, and UI concerns maintainable.
Screenshots show risk exposure, risk treatment performance, traffic-light monitoring, issues and next steps, KRI monitoring, approval management, and dashboard modules.
The platform unifies risk registers, treatment plans, KRI monitoring, action plans, multi-level approvals, and executive dashboards in one operational system.
result: Moved risk monitoring from spreadsheet-based tracking into a centralized platform with clearer ownership, structured approvals, KRI visibility, and real-time executive risk dashboards.

name: Automated Data Pipeline for Executive Power BI Dashboard
description: Containerized Airflow and Spark pipeline that extracts operational data, transforms metrics, loads a PostgreSQL data mart, and feeds executive Power BI dashboards.

screenshots.alt: Sanitized executive energy performance dashboard showing operational, risk, KPI, and financial metrics
Automated data pipeline architecture from source systems through Airflow, Spark, PostgreSQL data mart, and Power BI reporting

problem: Executive performance reporting depended on manual data preparation across multiple operational source systems. This made dashboard refreshes slower, harder to rerun safely, and less consistent across reporting periods.
solution: Built a containerized data pipeline for operational performance dashboards using Apache Airflow, Apache Spark, Docker, PostgreSQL, and MSSQL. The workflow automates extraction from multiple source systems, transforms operational metrics, validates and aggregates business logic, and loads dashboard-ready datasets into a centralized PostgreSQL data mart for Power BI.
proof: Designed the pipeline architecture, Airflow DAG scheduling, Spark ETL logic, SQL transformations, data validation, and rerun-safe refresh flow.
Pipeline supports scheduled refreshes, monthly updates, metric backfill handling, and centralized dashboard-ready tables.
Sanitized dashboard output shows executive-style performance views across operations, asset integrity, safety, risk, KPI, and financial metrics.
result: Reduced manual data preparation and improved the reliability, freshness, and consistency of dashboard reporting, giving executives a cleaner view of performance across reporting periods.

name: LNG Operations Visibility Dashboard
description: Operational gas monitoring dashboard that unified FSRU and onshore visibility with automated reporting, KPI trends, and executive-ready performance views.

screenshots.alt: Gas monitoring management dashboard showing supply, sales, boil-off gas, and performance gauges
Operational dashboard trend charts comparing targets and realization for supply and sales

problem: Gas operations reporting needed clearer visibility across FSRU and onshore facilities. Manual monitoring and fragmented reporting made it harder for operational and executive users to track supply, sales, boil-off gas, asset readiness, and KPI performance in one place.
solution: Built an operational gas monitoring dashboard that transforms LNG operations into a real-time visibility layer. The dashboard brings supply, sales, boil-off gas, ORF readiness, asset integrity, and trend views into one management interface for faster operational review.
proof: Public Rewire success story describes the project as transforming gas operations into a real-time intelligence platform.
Dashboard screenshots show management views for pasokan, penjualan, boil-off gas monitoring, performance gauges, and target-versus-realization trends.
The success story highlights unified monitoring across FSRU and onshore facilities, a 70% faster reporting cycle, and a real-time gas operation dashboard.
result: Improved operational visibility by giving stakeholders a centralized dashboard for gas operation monitoring, faster reporting cycles, and cleaner executive decision support.

name: LNG Corporate Website Platform
description: Corporate website revamp for a national LNG operator, turning a static web presence into a responsive, CMS-managed digital identity platform.

screenshots.alt: Nusantara Regas corporate website homepage hero showing FSRU infrastructure
Nusantara Regas homepage showing news, company profile, and business chain sections
Nusantara Regas business fields page showing LNG facilities and supply chain content

problem: A national LNG operator needed a stronger digital presence that reflected its role in Indonesia's LNG value chain. The existing website behaved like a static brochure: outdated design, limited mobile optimization, difficult content updates, and weak public visibility for stakeholders.
solution: Built a modern ASP.NET Core corporate website platform with a cleaner information architecture, LNG-focused visual design, responsive layouts, CMS-managed publishing, SEO-ready metadata, analytics integration, and content sections for company profile, business chain, commitments, communication, and stakeholder information.
proof: Public Rewire success story describes the project as moving from a static presence to a dynamic corporate platform.
The implementation uses ASP.NET Core with CMS-driven content management, responsive layouts, structured metadata, sitemap, and analytics.
Screenshots show a polished bilingual corporate interface with FSRU hero content, company profile sections, business-chain storytelling, news updates, and LNG business field pages.
result: Created a more credible and maintainable corporate digital identity, improving brand visibility, mobile access, content publishing, SEO discoverability, and public communication for a key LNG infrastructure operator.

name: 5TB Telco Data to BigQuery
description: Backend synchronization workflows for national-scale modem telemetry into BigQuery, supporting centralized analytics and near real-time reporting.
problem: National-scale modem telemetry needed to be synchronized into a centralized analytics environment. The data volume was large enough that simple exports and manual reporting patterns were not reliable for recurring operational insight.
solution: Designed backend data synchronization workflows that moved 5TB+ modem data into Google BigQuery. The system supported scheduled delivery, centralized analytics, reporting automation, and near real-time operational visibility.
proof: Handled 5TB+ of modem telemetry data.
Used cloud data infrastructure including AWS services and Google BigQuery.
Built scheduled data workflows with tools such as Apache Airflow and Apache Beam.
result: Enabled centralized analytics and more reliable reporting automation for national-scale telco operational data.

name: Nutricia Professional LMS
description: Doctor-facing learning platform for Danone's medical-professional program, delivered on schedule and scaled to thousands of users.
problem: Danone's medical-professional program needed a learning platform for doctors that could launch on schedule and support a large professional audience.
solution: Led a 3-person squad to deliver the LMS, coordinating backend delivery, deployment needs, and production readiness across the project lifecycle.
proof: Built with Next.js, Lumen, MySQL, AWS EC2, and AWS RDS.
Delivered for a real medical-professional audience rather than an internal-only demo.
Coordinated a small engineering squad through launch.
result: Launched on schedule and scaled to thousands of doctors.

name: Inventory Management System
description: Award-winning inventory platform replacing spreadsheet workflows with operational tracking, analytics, and reporting connectors.
problem: Inventory workflows depended heavily on spreadsheets, which made input slower, reporting harder, and operational tracking more error-prone.
solution: Built a web-based inventory management system with structured input flows, operational tracking, analytics support, and reporting connectors.
proof: Implemented as a Laravel/MySQL application with Blade views.
Designed around replacing spreadsheet-driven operational work.
Recognized through the Pertamina Sub-Holding Gas Continuous Improvement Program.
result: Reduced manual inventory handling and created a more reliable platform for operational tracking and reporting.

name: Policy Document Management System
description: Policy document workflow system for managing issued documents, secure transfer, and production document handling.
problem: Policy document handling needed a more reliable workflow for managing generated documents, delivery status, and transfer to downstream systems.
solution: Built a document management service using Next.js, ASP.NET Core, and SQL Server to support structured policy document handling and operational visibility.
proof: Uses a modern frontend with ASP.NET Core backend services.
Stores operational state in SQL Server.
Connects to real insurance document-delivery workflows.
result: Improved traceability, reduced manual coordination, and gave operations a more controlled way to monitor policy document readiness and delivery status.

name: E-Procurement Ecosystem
description: Procurement workflow ecosystem covering vendor, procurement, and approval flows for internal business operations.
problem: Procurement processes often involve fragmented requests, vendor coordination, approvals, and audit needs. Without a system, teams depend on manual tracking and scattered communication.
solution: Built an e-procurement ecosystem to centralize procurement workflows, vendor-related processes, and approval visibility.
proof: Implements a business workflow system with CodeIgniter, MySQL, and Bootstrap.
Designed around procurement process flows rather than a simple CRUD application.
Includes repository-accessible implementation details.
result: Created a structured platform for procurement operations and reduced reliance on disconnected manual tracking.

name: Portal Dashboard
description: Gas operations dashboard for internal operational visibility and backend workflow access.
problem: Gas operations teams needed a more centralized portal for operational data, internal workflows, and dashboard access.
solution: Built a gas operations portal using Blazor, ASP.NET Core, SQL Server, and dashboard-oriented UI patterns.
proof: Uses Microsoft-oriented backend and frontend tooling aligned with the enterprise environment.
Connects operational data and internal workflows through a dedicated portal.
Shares implementation through a public repository.
result: Improved operational visibility by giving gas operations users a centralized dashboard and workflow entry point.

name: Tokopedia Scraper API
description: API interface for scraping product data from Tokopedia using Python, Flask, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, and PostgreSQL.
problem: Product research and market analysis can require repeatable extraction of product information from marketplace pages. Manual checking is slow and hard to reuse.
solution: Built a scraper API using Python, Flask, BeautifulSoup, Selenium, and PostgreSQL to expose product scraping through a backend interface.
proof: Combines browser automation and HTML parsing for marketplace data extraction.
Stores extracted data in PostgreSQL.
Exposes the workflow through an API rather than a one-off script.
result: Turned marketplace product extraction into a reusable backend service.
